data.title: Cathie Wood says Bitcoin is solution for incoming AI deflation chaos
data.description:

Cathie Wood spoke with Anthony Pompliano at Bitcoin Investor Week to discuss a myriad of different economic topics. The focal point of their conversation, however, was centered around what […]

data.title: SEC Unveils “Project Crypto” to Replace Enforcement-First Model With Clear Asset Rules
data.description:
The Division of Corporation Finance at the U. S. Securities and Exchange Commission, led by Director James Moloney, is advancing a new regulatory framework for digital assets under an initiative titled “Project Crypto. ” Announced on February 13, 2026, the proposal aims to replace the prior enforcement-heavy posture with structured, rule-based guidance
source: https://www.ethnews.com/sec-unveils-project-crypto-to-replace-enforcement-first-model-with-clear-asset-rules/

data.title: BOE to Monitor Economic Data Following Rate Decision
data.description: The Bank of England (BOE) is set to closely observe upcoming economic data after its recent decision to maintain interest rates, driven by concerns over persistently high inflation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the BOE's cautious approach amid inflationary pressures. The decision to keep rates unchanged was finely balanced, reflecting the central bank's focus on economic indicators that could influence future monetary policy. The BOE's vigilance comes as it seeks to navigate the challenges posed by inflation while supporting economic stability.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million New Tokens Amid Rising Demand
data.description: The USDC Treasury has minted 250 million new USDC tokens, according to Whale Alert. This substantial issuance boosts the circulating supply of the USDC stablecoin. According to NS3.AI, the move may suggest an increasing demand for stable liquidity within the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | German Chancellor Calls for Strengthening Transatlantic Relations
data.description:
German Chancellor Merz stated at the Munich Security Conference last Friday that the rules-based international order "no longer exists." According to Ming Pao, Merz agreed with U.S. Vice President Vance's previous assertion at the same event that there are "deep rifts" between Europe and the United States. Despite this, Merz urged both sides to work together to repair and restore trust across the Atlantic.

Merz emphasized the need for Europe to reduce its excessive reliance on the United States for defense. He disclosed that he is engaged in confidential discussions with French President Macron about establishing a joint nuclear deterrence system in Europe.

data.title: U.S. Government Subpoenas Tech Firms for User Data on ICE Critics
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Homeland Security has issued numerous subpoenas to social media companies, including Meta, Google, Reddit, and Discord, over recent months. According to Ming Pao, these subpoenas demand personal information such as names, emails, and phone numbers of users who criticize the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) while using anonymous accounts.

Tech companies have stated that they review government requests before deciding whether to comply. Some companies also notify the individuals involved, giving them 10 to 14 days to contest the subpoenas. The Department of Homeland Security claims that the data requests are intended to ensure the safety of ICE personnel.

The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) has criticized the current administration, accusing it of being more aggressive than its predecessor. The ACLU argues that the subpoenas allow the Department of Homeland Security to bypass judicial oversight and exert pressure on individuals.

data.title: Something Big Is Coming to Your X Feed, and It Involves Bitcoin

source: https://techgaged.com/something-big-is-coming-to-your-x-feed-and-it-involves-bitcoin/
